pattern: Only the left half is free, so the k-th palindrome is the k-th distinct multiset permutation of that half, found by counting completions char by char with a capped multinomial.
hints: A distinct palindrome corresponds one-to-one with a distinct arrangement of its left half (plus the forced middle), so count and index those half-arrangements.
At each position try letters in ascending order; the number of ways to fill the rest is the multinomial of the remaining half-multiset, which you can cap at k to avoid huge numbers.

If k exceeds that block of completions, subtract it and try the next letter; otherwise fix this letter and recurse. If k still remains after the whole half, there are fewer than k palindromes so return an empty string.

edge_inputs.description: first arrangement with a middle letter
k larger than the total distinct count
odd length, middle fixed, second arrangement

stress.description: small multisets where k ranges across and beyond the full distinct count
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    half_len = rng.randrange(1, max(2, size // 2 + 1))
    half = [chr(ord('a') + rng.randrange(4)) for _ in range(half_len)]
    mid = chr(ord('a') + rng.randrange(4)) if rng.random() < 0.5 else ''
    s = ''.join(half) + mid + ''.join(reversed(half))
    k = rng.randrange(1, 200)
    return [s, k]
stress.note: tiny halves so the brute oracle can enumerate all distinct palindromes
